attacking_style: Launches punchy accelerations 500-800m from summit finishes on steep gradients, excelling at late-climb surges rather than long-range efforts. Most effective with explosive final-kilometer attacks from breakaways or reduced groups.
team_tactics: Often operates with minimal team support in finales, relying on personal positioning and opportunistic moves. Team occasionally commits early for KOM points but Woods typically isolated on decisive climbs, forcing self-reliant tactical decisions.
terrain_preference: Summit finishes on steep percentage climbs (Mur de Huy, Puy de Dôme-style). Vulnerable on technical descents and flat positioning where crashes occur repeatedly. Altitude climbing improves throughout stage races but lacks acceleration to match pure GC climbers on extended HC ascents.
vulnerability: Chronic crash risk on flat roads, narrow technical sections, and descents (6+ incidents documented). Exposed when multiple explosive accelerations required; can follow initial moves but cracks on repeated surges. Going too early on Mur de Huy (200m) leaves him vulnerable to late movers.
key_patterns: Crashes on flat/technical terrain compromise stage racing objectives repeatedly (Vuelta 2022 exit, Tour 2022, Vuelta 2020, 2018)
Consistently places 4th-6th at Fleche Wallonne without winning acceleration on steepest Mur gradients (2019-2023)
Ambushes breakaway companions in final kilometer rather than cooperating to finish (Tirreno 2020, Vuelta 2018)
KOM jersey hunting with early breakaway commitment shows secondary objective focus beyond GC (Tour 2021)
Superior finishing speed but compromised by poor final positioning in sprint scenarios (Vuelta 2020 corner)
form_trajectory: positive: Age 36 victory at Puy de Dôme (2023) with exceptional late-surge ability demonstrates sustained punchy climbing form despite crashes earlier in career. Consistent Monument and one-day results suggest maintained peak rather than decline.
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3
